actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can request bounded workspace search and bounded database query. Can prepare page creation, page update, and comment previews when explicit parent, page, database, workspace, or query context is supplied. Should be asked for clarification when required context is missing or ambiguous.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: May request preview-only page creation, page update, and comment preparation, but should not be treated as having authority to apply or post mutations unless separately approved.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Business user who interacts with the governed Notion fronting service for bounded search, database query, and preparation of page or comment changes.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can see only actor-visible summaries within allowed workspace, page, and database scope. Must not receive raw workspace export, hidden page access, or private page exfiltration.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can review prepared page creation, page update, and comment previews and decide whether the requested mutation may proceed.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Holds approval authority for preview-only mutation flows, but the exact organizational role and whether authority differs by action type are not specified in the source.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Business authority who reviews prepared page creations, page updates, and comments before any mutation is allowed.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Should see the prepared preview, relevant bounded context, and approval record needed to make a decision, without expanding access into raw workspace export or hidden content.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can review whether bounded access, allowlists, approval records, and denial behavior are operating as intended within the fronting service.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: May or may not be the same person as the mutation approver; the source confirms audit and approval records exist but does not define whether this actor can directly approve changes.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Oversight actor responsible for reviewing audit and approval records for the centralized governed fronting service.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Should see approval records, audit evidence, and scope enforcement behavior relevant to governance review, but not gain unrestricted workspace content access by default.

service_topology_bindings.implementation_notes:
Accepted assistant guidance:
- Formalize the capability surface each service must expose: Define the stable capability contracts each service is expected to own, including which actions are read-only, which require approval, and which must stop for clarification.
  Why: The generator and verifier need stable capability ownership, not only narrative service descriptions.
- Describe the required coordination edges between services: Capture where one service depends on another for bounded context, approvals, or downstream execution so coordination can be represented explicitly instead of as hidden glue.
  Why: Coordination edges are easier to validate when they are declared before implementation.
- Keep approval boundaries outside the main happy path: Identify which service should prepare a proposed action and which surface or role must approve it before the mutation boundary is crossed.
  Why: Approval semantics should be explicit in service design rather than buried in one implementation path.
- Preserve explanation and traceability across service boundaries: Define which service boundaries must preserve explanation, lineage, or durable evidence so later verification can reconstruct why a result or stop condition occurred.
  Why: Cross-service traceability should be deliberate, not inferred after implementation exists.

product_alignment.approval_posture_formalization: Read operations can proceed within bounded visible scope. Any page creation, page update, or comment action is preview-only and approval-gated before mutation, with approval records and audit expected as part of the service posture.
product_alignment.governed_behavior_formalization: The product exposes only bounded Notion capabilities. Reads are limited to actor-visible workspace, page, database, and result scope and return summaries rather than raw exports. Missing required context returns clarification-required. Page creation, page updates, and comments stop at preview and require approval before any mutation. Private page exfiltration, hidden page access, raw export, arbitrary block mutation, workspace administration, and unapproved mutations are denied.

sections.description: How restrictions, denials, approvals, and clarification posture become formal service behavior.
sections.label: Authority & Approval

linked_product_design_items.detail: Read operations can proceed within bounded visible scope. Any page creation, page update, or comment action is preview-only and approval-gated before mutation, with approval records and audit expected as part of the service posture.
linked_product_design_items.label: Approval Posture Summary

linked_product_design_items.detail: May request preview-only page creation, page update, and comment preparation, but should not be treated as having authority to apply or post mutations unless separately approved.
linked_product_design_items.label: Governed Notion Requester approval expectations

linked_product_design_items.detail: Holds approval authority for preview-only mutation flows, but the exact organizational role and whether authority differs by action type are not specified in the source.
linked_product_design_items.label: Notion Change Approver approval expectations

linked_product_design_items.detail: May or may not be the same person as the mutation approver; the source confirms audit and approval records exist but does not define whether this actor can directly approve changes.
linked_product_design_items.label: Notion Governance Reviewer approval expectations

linked_product_design_items.detail: Bounded · Bounded Result · Allow bounded workspace search within declared workspace scope and return actor-visible summaries only, not raw workspace tools or exports.
linked_product_design_items.label: Governed Notion Requester · Workspace Context Search

linked_product_design_items.detail: Bounded · Bounded Result · Allow bounded database query against an explicit database and return summarized context rather than raw export.
linked_product_design_items.label: Governed Notion Requester · Database Context Query

linked_product_design_items.detail: Approval Required · Approval Stop · Allow preparation of a page creation preview under an explicit parent, then stop for approval before creation.
linked_product_design_items.label: Governed Notion Requester · Page Creation Preparation

linked_product_design_items.detail: Approval Required · Approval Stop · Allow preparation of a page update preview for an explicit page, then stop for approval before applying changes.
linked_product_design_items.label: Governed Notion Requester · Page Update Preparation

linked_product_design_items.detail: Approval Required · Approval Stop · Allow preparation of a page comment preview from supplied context, then stop for approval before posting.
linked_product_design_items.label: Governed Notion Requester · Comment Preparation

linked_product_design_items.detail: Restricted · Clarification Required · Stop and request clarification when workspace scope, query, page id, parent id, or database id is missing or ambiguous.
linked_product_design_items.label: Governed Notion Requester · Clarification Handling

linked_product_design_items.detail: Denied · Deny Request · Deny requests for hidden page access, private page exfiltration, raw workspace export, arbitrary block mutation, parent override, or workspace-wide unrestricted access.
linked_product_design_items.label: Governed Notion Requester · Scope and Visibility Controls

linked_product_design_items.detail: Denied · Deny Request · Deny direct mutation attempts when approval has not been granted for page creation, page update, or comment posting.
linked_product_design_items.label: Governed Notion Requester · Approval-Gated Changes

linked_product_design_items.detail: Allowed · Direct Result · Allow review and approval decision handling for prepared page creation, page update, and comment previews.
linked_product_design_items.label: Notion Change Approver · Approval-Gated Changes

linked_product_design_items.detail: Allowed · Direct Result · Allow governance review of approval records, scope enforcement posture, and audit-relevant outcomes for the governed Notion fronting service.
linked_product_design_items.label: Notion Governance Reviewer · Policy Enforcement and Audit

linked_product_design_items.detail: Business goal the implementation should help achieve.
linked_product_design_items.label: Make Clarification, Denial, Approval Records, And Audit Part Of The Operating Model.

linked_product_design_items.detail: The product exposes only bounded Notion capabilities. Reads are limited to actor-visible workspace, page, database, and result scope and return summaries rather than raw exports. Missing required context returns clarification-required. Page creation, page updates, and comments stop at preview and require approval before any mutation. Private page exfiltration, hidden page access, raw export, arbitrary block mutation, workspace administration, and unapproved mutations are denied.
linked_product_design_items.label: Governed Behavior Summary

package.readme:
# Notion Fronting Showcase

ANIP package `notion-fronting-showcase@0.2.0` for local showcase and registry smoke usage.

## Contents

- Services: 1
- Capabilities: 5
- ANIP spec: `anip/0.24`

## Capability Surface

- `notion.workspace.search_context`
- `notion.database.query_context`
- `notion.page.create.prepare`
- `notion.page.update.prepare`
- `notion.comment.prepare`

## Generate

From a downloaded package bundle:

```bash
go run ./cmd/anip-generate --package-bundle <downloaded-package>.anip-package.json --target python --dependency-source local --output ./generated/notion-fronting-showcase --force
```

From a trusted registry package:

```bash
go run ./cmd/anip-generate --registry-url <registry-url> --package-id notion-fronting-showcase --package-version 0.2.0 --target python --dependency-source registry --output ./generated/notion-fronting-showcase --force
```

## Verify

```bash
go run ./cmd/anip-verify --definition anip-service-definition.json
go run ./cmd/anip-verify --package-bundle <downloaded-package>.anip-package.json
```

## Run Locally

Generated services default to port `9162` unless overridden by the generated runtime configuration.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can request bounded workspace search and bounded database query. Can prepare page creation, page update, and comment previews when explicit parent, page, database, workspace, or query context is supplied. Should be asked for clarification when required context is missing or ambiguous.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: May request preview-only page creation, page update, and comment preparation, but should not be treated as having authority to apply or post mutations unless separately approved.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Business user who interacts with the governed Notion fronting service for bounded search, database query, and preparation of page or comment changes.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can see only actor-visible summaries within allowed workspace, page, and database scope. Must not receive raw workspace export, hidden page access, or private page exfiltration.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can review prepared page creation, page update, and comment previews and decide whether the requested mutation may proceed.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Holds approval authority for preview-only mutation flows, but the exact organizational role and whether authority differs by action type are not specified in the source.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Business authority who reviews prepared page creations, page updates, and comments before any mutation is allowed.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Should see the prepared preview, relevant bounded context, and approval record needed to make a decision, without expanding access into raw workspace export or hidden content.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can review whether bounded access, allowlists, approval records, and denial behavior are operating as intended within the fronting service.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: May or may not be the same person as the mutation approver; the source confirms audit and approval records exist but does not define whether this actor can directly approve changes.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Oversight actor responsible for reviewing audit and approval records for the centralized governed fronting service.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Should see approval records, audit evidence, and scope enforcement behavior relevant to governance review, but not gain unrestricted workspace content access by default.

service_topology_bindings.implementation_notes:
Accepted assistant guidance:
- Formalize the capability surface each service must expose: Define the stable capability contracts each service is expected to own, including which actions are read-only, which require approval, and which must stop for clarification.
  Why: The generator and verifier need stable capability ownership, not only narrative service descriptions.
- Describe the required coordination edges between services: Capture where one service depends on another for bounded context, approvals, or downstream execution so coordination can be represented explicitly instead of as hidden glue.
  Why: Coordination edges are easier to validate when they are declared before implementation.
- Keep approval boundaries outside the main happy path: Identify which service should prepare a proposed action and which surface or role must approve it before the mutation boundary is crossed.
  Why: Approval semantics should be explicit in service design rather than buried in one implementation path.
- Preserve explanation and traceability across service boundaries: Define which service boundaries must preserve explanation, lineage, or durable evidence so later verification can reconstruct why a result or stop condition occurred.
  Why: Cross-service traceability should be deliberate, not inferred after implementation exists.

product_alignment.approval_posture_formalization: Read operations can proceed within bounded visible scope. Any page creation, page update, or comment action is preview-only and approval-gated before mutation, with approval records and audit expected as part of the service posture.
product_alignment.governed_behavior_formalization: The product exposes only bounded Notion capabilities. Reads are limited to actor-visible workspace, page, database, and result scope and return summaries rather than raw exports. Missing required context returns clarification-required. Page creation, page updates, and comments stop at preview and require approval before any mutation. Private page exfiltration, hidden page access, raw export, arbitrary block mutation, workspace administration, and unapproved mutations are denied.

linked_product_design_items.detail: Business goal the implementation should help achieve.
linked_product_design_items.label: Make Clarification, Denial, Approval Records, And Audit Part Of The Operating Model.

linked_product_design_items.detail: The product exposes only bounded Notion capabilities. Reads are limited to actor-visible workspace, page, database, and result scope and return summaries rather than raw exports. Missing required context returns clarification-required. Page creation, page updates, and comments stop at preview and require approval before any mutation. Private page exfiltration, hidden page access, raw export, arbitrary block mutation, workspace administration, and unapproved mutations are denied.
linked_product_design_items.label: Governed Behavior Summary

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Representative reviews show bounded workspace search and database query flows succeed only within declared scope and return summarized context, not raw export output.
success_criteria_checks.review_method: Scenario review and integration evidence review
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Users can search allowed Notion workspace context and query allowed databases through governed capabilities that return actor-visible summaries rather than raw workspace exports.
success_criteria_checks.verification_strategy: Scenario review and integration evidence review

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Representative create, update, and comment scenarios show preview artifacts are produced, approval is required before execution, and no unapproved mutation occurs.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Page creation, page update, and comment workflows stop at preview and require approval before any Notion mutation is applied.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Scenario review shows missing or ambiguous identifiers and query context produce explicit clarification-required outcomes across read and preparation flows.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: When required workspace, page, parent, database, or query inputs are missing or ambiguous, the service returns clarification-required instead of guessing or overreaching.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Policy and scenario review demonstrate explicit denial outcomes for representative prohibited requests rather than partial execution or silent fallback.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Unsafe or out-of-policy requests are denied, including private page exfiltration, hidden page access, raw workspace export, workspace administration, arbitrary block mutation, and unapproved changes.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Interface and integration review confirm the agent-facing surface is limited to the governed capability set backed by native Notion API implementation.
success_criteria_checks.review_method: Surface review and integration evidence review
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Agents interact only with governed Notion capabilities and are not exposed to raw Notion API, MCP, or unrestricted workspace tools.
success_criteria_checks.verification_strategy: Surface review and integration evidence review
